task.name: Validate Workflow Output
task.objective: Validate a workflow's output against its checklist/validation schema
task.description: This task is invoked by workflows that have a validation step. It loads the specified checklist and validates the workflow's output file against it, reporting any issues found.

inputs.input: Path to the validation checklist file
Path to the output file to validate (auto-detected if not provided)
Path to the workflow.yaml for variable context

step.title: Resolve Scope Context
step.objective: Resolve active scope before loading any paths, using the same priority chain as workflow.xml Step 0

substep.title: Resolve Scope (PARALLEL-SAFE PRIORITY ORDER)

priority-order.desc: Check these sources IN ORDER - first match wins

source.name: Inline --scope argument
source.action: Check if command invocation includes a scope parameter
If found, store as {scope} and echo: "**[SCOPE: {scope}]** Using inline scope"

source.name: Conversation memory
source.action: Check if scope was set earlier in THIS conversation
If remembered, use it and echo: "**[SCOPE: {scope}]** Using scope from conversation context"

source.name: BMAD_SCOPE environment variable
source.action: If BMAD_SCOPE is set, use its value as scope
Echo: "**[SCOPE: {scope}]** Using scope from BMAD_SCOPE env var"

source.name: Session context file (.bmad-scope)
source.action: If exists at project root, parse YAML
If enabled: false OR disabled: true → IGNORE (treat as not found)
If enabled AND active_scope present → use active_scope value
Echo: "**[SCOPE: {scope}]** Using scope from .bmad-scope (shared file - not parallel-safe)"

substep.action: If no scope from any source, echo: "**[NO SCOPE]** Running without scope isolation"

substep.title: Apply Scope Path Overrides (if scope is set)

check.action: Override path variables for the rest of this task: - {scope_path} = {output_folder}/{scope} - {planning_artifacts} = {scope_path}/planning-artifacts (OVERRIDE config.yaml!) - {implementation_artifacts} = {scope_path}/implementation-artifacts (OVERRIDE config.yaml!) - {scope_tests} = {scope_path}/tests
check.note: config.yaml contains static pre-resolved paths from install time. When scope is active, use the overridden values above.

step.title: Load Validation Context
step.action: Load the checklist file from the provided checklist_path
If workflow_path provided, load workflow.yaml to get variable context

Identify the output file to validate: - If output_file provided explicitly, use it (callers may pre-resolve scope paths and pass output_file directly) - Else check workflow.yaml for default_output_file; if it contains {planning_artifacts}, {implementation_artifacts}, or {scope_tests}, substitute the scoped paths resolved in Step 0 - Else ask user to specify the file to validate

step.title: Parse Checklist Requirements
step.action: Read the checklist thoroughly to understand ALL validation criteria
Identify: - Required sections that must exist - Quality criteria that must be met - Anti-patterns to check for - Completeness requirements
Create a mental checklist of ALL items to verify

step.title: Systematic Validation
step.action: Go through EACH checklist item systematically
For each requirement: - Check if the output file satisfies it - Note any missing, incomplete, or incorrect content - Record specific line numbers or sections with issues
Check for anti-patterns mentioned in the checklist
Verify completeness - no placeholder text, no TODO items left

step.title: Generate Validation Report
step.action: Compile findings into a structured report

step.title: Present Results and Offer Fixes
step.action: Display the validation report to user

check.ask: Would you like me to attempt to fix these issues? - [Y] Yes, fix the issues automatically - [N] No, I'll fix them manually - [R] Re-run validation after I make changes

check.action: Attempt to fix each issue in the output file
Echo: "✅ Validation PASSED - output meets all checklist requirements"

protocol.name: thorough_validation
protocol.rule: Never skip checklist items - validate EVERY requirement
Be specific about issues - include line numbers and exact problems
Provide actionable feedback - don't just say "incomplete", say what's missing
